label: Localized hair dye rash without eye or systemic red flags
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of localized hair dye exposure skin reaction.
No anaphylaxis, systemic allergic reaction, mucosal involvement, skin infection, severe medication reaction, eye involvement, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Anaphylaxis, airway symptoms, hypotension, systemic allergic reaction, mucosal lesions, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, skin infection, rapidly progressive rash, burn or chemical exposure, ocular symptoms or periorbital concern, immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed dermatology/allergy plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, dermatology, allergy, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports contact dermatitis framing from irritant or allergic exposures, trigger avoidance, gentle skin care, clinician-directed topical or allergy treatment, and escalation for severe allergic, infectious, ocular, or mucosal red flags. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed localized hair dye exposure skin reaction with anaphylaxis, infection, mucosal, and severe-rash red flags absent.

terms: clinician diagnosis localized hair dye rash
discharge diagnosis localized hair dye rash

high_confidence_terms: clinician diagnosis localized hair dye rash

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. no infection, mucosal, or severe-rash red flags
